values: Frozen Beas Kund surrounded by snow in mid-June, ice begins to thaw as July descends
Snow patches on the ground in summer despite it being summer
Meadows emerge as monsoon season deepens
Frozen Beas Kund melting in small concentric circles in mid-July, ice thinning, meadows around lake emerging past mid-July
Lush greenery replacing snow from mid-August to September
Beas Kund flaunts deep royal blue colour with green grass and flowers blooming around it in mid-August to September
Monsoon receding with blue skies peeping out, arresting views of underrated mountains of Himachal Pradesh
Views of Dhauladhar range with seven sisters, Hanuman Tibba, Mt Manali, and Shitidhar from Lohali camp under brilliant blue sky as autumn emerges in September

evidence.quote_or_summary: The most prominent being a frozen Beas Kund, surrounded by snow. The ice begins to thaw only as July descends.
Even though the meadows are a highlight of this trek, they emerge only as the monsoon season deepens.
The frozen Beas Kund has also started melting in small concentric circles. Parts of it are still icy, but the ice is thinning. Meadows around the lake also start emerging as you pass mid-July.
The snow is gone. Beas Kund flaunts a deep royal blue colour with green grass and flowers blooming around it. It is a sight to behold.
Monsoon is receding now and blue skies have started peeping out. It’s a great time to catch the arresting views of the underrated mountains of Himachal Pradesh.

When you camp at Lohali, you see the Dhauladhar range rising tall with the seven sisters, Hanuman Tibba, Mt Manali, and Shitidhar among many others. These views sparkle under the brilliant blue sky as autumn emerges in September.

values: Snow present at Palchhani Thatch and on trek in mid-June, traces of winter lingering
Snow patches on the ground in summer (mid-June to mid-July)
Some snow on final ascend to Beas Kund in early July, most snow melted by mid-July
Snow gone from mid-August to September

evidence.quote_or_summary: At the beginning of the season, around mid-June, you will find a lot of snow at Palchhani Thatch. The trek is just starting to emerge from the snow. During this time, you will find many traces of winter lingering on the trek.
Despite it being summer, expect snow patches on the ground.
You still find some snow on your final ascend to Beas Kund, especially in early July, but most snow on the trek has melted.
The snow is gone, and the terrain is replaced with lush greenery.

values: Heavy rain in the afternoon from mid-July to mid-August; carrying a poncho is compulsory
Monsoon at its peak from mid-July to mid-August with frequent rains and unannounced clouds shrouding the lake and trail
Monsoon receding from mid-August to September

evidence.quote_or_summary: Expect heavy rain in the afternoon. Carrying a poncho is compulsory.
Now, the monsoon is also at its peak. Rains become frequent and clouds usually glide in unannounced shrouding the lake and the trail.
Monsoon is receding now and blue skies have started peeping out.

values: Mid-June to mid-July: Day 15-20 °C, Night 1-5 °C
Mid-July to mid-August: Day 13-18 °C, Night 1-5 °C
Mid-August to September: Day 12-15 °C, Night 0-3 °C
4 warm layers recommended across all three windows; cold subject to wind, moisture and other factors

evidence.quote_or_summary: Temperature in Summer: Day time: Between 15 °C and 20 °C | Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 1 °C and 5 °C.
Day time: Between 13 °C and 18 °C | Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 1 °C and 5 °C.
Day time: Between 12 °C and 15 °C | Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 0 °C and 3 °C.
Number of warm layers recommended in Summer: 4 layers (cold is subject to wind, moisture and other factors.)

evidence.quote_or_summary: Even though the meadows are a highlight of this trek, they emerge only as the monsoon season deepens.
Beas Kund flaunts a deep royal blue colour with green grass and flowers blooming around it. It is a sight to behold.

When you camp at Lohali, you see the Dhauladhar range rising tall with the seven sisters, Hanuman Tibba, Mt Manali, and Shitidhar among many others. These views sparkle under the brilliant blue sky as autumn emerges in September.
